definitions.definition: A matrix is a rectangular array of numbers, arranged in rows and columns, enclosed by square brackets [] or round brackets (). The numbers in the matrix are called elements or entries.
The order of a matrix is the number of rows and columns it has, denoted as mxn, where m is the number of rows and n is the number of columns.
definitions.example: The order of matrix A = [1 2; 3 4] is 2x2.

definitions.definition: A row matrix is a matrix with only one row, i.e., its order is 1xn.
A column matrix is a matrix with only one column, i.e., its order is mx1.
A square matrix is a matrix with the same number of rows and columns, i.e., its order is mxm.
A diagonal matrix is a square matrix with all non-zero elements on the main diagonal and zeros elsewhere.
An identity matrix is a square matrix with ones on the main diagonal and zeros elsewhere, denoted as I.
A zero matrix is a matrix with all elements equal to zero, denoted as O.
Two matrices are said to be equal if they have the same order and their corresponding elements are equal.
definitions.example: A = [1 2; 3 4] and B = [1 2; 3 4] are equal matrices.

definitions.definition: The sum of two matrices A and B is a matrix obtained by adding the corresponding elements of A and B.
The difference of two matrices A and B is a matrix obtained by subtracting the corresponding elements of B from A.

definitions.term: Multiplication of a Matrix by a Scalar
definitions.definition: The product of a matrix A and a scalar k is a matrix obtained by multiplying each element of A by k.
The product of two matrices A and B is a matrix obtained by multiplying the rows of A by the columns of B.
The transpose of a matrix A is a matrix obtained by interchanging the rows and columns of A, denoted as A' or AT.
A symmetric matrix is a square matrix that is equal to its transpose, i.e., A = A'.
A skew-symmetric matrix is a square matrix that is equal to the negative of its transpose, i.e., A = -A'.
The inverse of a square matrix A is a matrix B such that AB = BA = I, denoted as A^-1.
